rules.name: AWS Access Key Detected
rules.description: Potential hardcoded AWS access key detected.
rules.remediation: Move credentials to environment variables or a secret manager.

rules.name: Private Key Material
rules.description: Private key header found in source files.
rules.remediation: Remove keys from source control and rotate immediately.

rules.name: JWT-like Token
rules.description: JWT-like token string found in code.
rules.remediation: Avoid hardcoding tokens in code and rotate compromised tokens.

rules.name: Hardcoded Password Assignment
rules.description: Password-like variable assigned directly in code.
rules.remediation: Store credentials outside source code and inject at runtime.

rules.name: API Token Assignment
rules.description: Token-like variable assigned to a static value.
rules.remediation: Use environment variables and rotate exposed tokens.

rules.name: Database Connection String
rules.description: Hardcoded database connection URI found.
rules.remediation: Move database URLs to secure configuration storage.

rules.name: Email Credential Exposure
rules.description: SMTP credentials appear hardcoded.
rules.remediation: Store email credentials in secret storage.

rules.name: Use of eval()
rules.description: Dynamic code execution through eval().
rules.remediation: Replace eval with safe parsing or explicit mapping.

rules.name: Use of exec()
rules.description: Dynamic code execution through exec().
rules.remediation: Avoid exec or isolate execution with strict validation.

rules.name: Use of os.system()
rules.description: Shell command execution through os.system().
rules.remediation: Use subprocess with argument lists and no shell invocation.

rules.name: subprocess with shell=True
rules.description: subprocess call enables shell execution.
rules.remediation: Use shell=False and pass explicit argument arrays.

rules.name: pickle deserialization
rules.description: Potential unsafe deserialization using pickle.
rules.remediation: Avoid untrusted pickle data; use safe formats like JSON.

rules.name: Unsafe yaml.load
rules.description: yaml.load used without SafeLoader.
rules.remediation: Use yaml.safe_load or specify SafeLoader explicitly.

rules.name: Weak Hash Function
rules.description: Use of md5/sha1 for security-sensitive hashing.
rules.remediation: Use SHA-256+ or dedicated password hash functions.

rules.name: Hardcoded Credentials in Python
rules.description: Python assignment likely contains static credentials.
rules.remediation: Load credentials from secure runtime sources.

rules.name: Broad Exception Handling
rules.description: Broad exception handler may hide security failures.
rules.remediation: Catch specific exception types and handle failures explicitly.

rules.name: Possible Command Injection
rules.description: Dynamic command construction detected in shell execution path.
rules.remediation: Sanitize inputs and avoid shell command interpolation.

rules.name: Possible SQL Injection
rules.description: SQL query appears built via string interpolation.
rules.remediation: Use parameterized queries with bound parameters.

rules.name: Unpinned Dependency
rules.description: Dependency does not use strict version pinning.
rules.remediation: Pin dependencies with exact versions to improve supply chain safety.

rules.name: Known Risky Dependency
rules.description: Dependency appears in local risk database with vulnerable range.
rules.remediation: Upgrade to a safe version and verify transitive dependencies.
